title: Cache Poisoning Safeguard Flow
description: A build-and-CI governance workflow that enforces stage gates, capacity policies, and quality/security controls before pipeline advancement. Focus case: cache poisoning safeguard flow.
assumptions: Operator credentials and reviewer tokens are unique and non-transferable during evaluation steps
Promotion policies are authoritative and consistent across pipeline variants
Cache nodes expose an allocation/claim API represented by reservation predicates
Automated quality and security checks produce deterministic yes/no results for gating decisions
Governance_group capacity constraints are enforced externally and represented by approver-role relations
risks: False negatives in automated scans or tests may allow poisoned artifacts to be promoted
Insufficient reviewer throughput or approver availability can create promotion bottlenecks
Misconfigured promotion_policy bindings could permit lateral promotion bypassing intended gates
Credential compromise (operator_credential) could enable unauthorized verification or promotion
Race conditions in cache_node reservation and release may leave stale or poisoned artifacts available
